metadata.title: ExampleCo Medium Family Overlap Mapping
metadata.remarks: Synthetic family-level mapping seed. This file does not reproduce framework text and does not claim exact clause, article, or control equivalence.

provenance.matching-rationale: The public ExampleCo medium seed supports family-level mappings from synthetic OpenCompliance controls to proxy ISO 27001, SOC 2, GDPR, IRAP, NIST CSF 2.0, Cyber Essentials, NCSC CAF 4.0, NIST SP 800-53 Rev. 5.1, GLBA Safeguards Rule, NYDFS 23 NYCRR Part 500, CCPA/CPRA, HIPAA Breach Notification Rule, HIPAA Privacy Rule targets.
provenance.mapping-description: Maps the synthetic OpenCompliance medium corridor controls to public-safe proxy identifiers representing overlapping framework families.

maps.matching-rationale: Scoped administrative MFA enforcement is one narrow synthetic OpenCompliance slice of the broader identity_and_access_management, access_control, security_of_processing, identity_access, identity_management_and_access_control, user_access_control, identity_and_access_control, identification_and_authentication, access_controls_and_mfa, and multi_factor_authentication families.

Unique infrastructure access identities required is one narrow synthetic OpenCompliance slice of the broader access_control, identity_and_access_management, identity_access, security_of_processing, user_access_control, identity_and_access_control, identity_management_and_access_control, and identification_and_authentication families.

Customer and environment segmentation enforced is one narrow synthetic OpenCompliance slice of the broader environment_and_tenant_separation, segregation_and_environment_separation, network_segmentation_and_isolation, security_of_processing, system_security_and_segregation, segmentation_and_isolation, and system_partitioning_and_boundary_protection families.

Scoped password policy enforced is one narrow synthetic OpenCompliance slice of the broader access_control, identity_and_access_management, identity_access, security_of_processing, user_access_control, identity_and_access_control, identity_management_and_access_control, and authenticator_management families.
